description: Prologis is a global industrial REIT that owns, develops, and operates logistics real estate while layering in adjacent energy, mobility, workforce, and operational services for warehouse customers.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: Prologis is one of the largest global owners and operators of logistics real estate, with a portfolio centered on warehouses, distribution facilities, and industrial land in major consumption and supply-chain markets. Its core business is still refreshingly concrete: own strategically located boxes, lease them to companies that need to move goods, and extract value from scale, location, and customer stickiness.

The company serves retailers, manufacturers, logistics providers, and e-commerce operators whose operations depend on being near transportation corridors and dense population centers. That makes Prologis less a speculative technology platform than a physical infrastructure landlord with a very good view of what modern commerce is actually doing.

overview.title: Products and Services
paragraphs: The primary product is logistics real estate: warehouse space, development capacity, build-to-suit projects, leasing, property management, and portfolio services. Prologis uses its global footprint and customer relationships to capture demand for modern distribution space, particularly where land is scarce and permitting is slow.

Prologis Essentials extends the relationship beyond rent collection into operational add-ons such as racking, material handling, energy and sustainability projects, fleet charging, workforce support, and related services. The strategic logic is straightforward: once the customer is inside the warehouse, Prologis would prefer not to leave the rest of the operating budget unattended.

overview.title: Strategic Position
paragraphs: Prologis benefits from structural demand for logistics capacity, urban infill locations, and supply-chain resiliency, but it remains exposed to interest rates, tenant demand cycles, development economics, and capital-market access. As a REIT, its cost of capital and asset valuations matter almost as much as warehouse utilization.

The company has increasingly framed its platform around logistics real estate, energy, digital infrastructure, and private capital. For Free The World mapping, Prologis is best treated as a real-asset infrastructure company with service extensions rather than a pure software or technology-wave company.
